items.title: An 'incalculable' number of corpses are in Venezuela's earthquake zone. People feel abandoned

children.content: On the ground in Venezuela death and desperation linger, but they are juxtaposed against miracle survival stories.

articles.title: Venezuela earthquake survivor pulled from rubble after eight days

children.content: A 43 year old man has been pulled from the rubble of a collapsed shopping centre after being trapped for eight days after earthquakes in Venezuela.

image.altText: A man on a stretcher with an oxygen mask and orange blanket

items.title: For decades Australian house prices have grown. This is what a fall could mean

children.content: After decades of growth Australian house prices are slowing. Here's what a fall could look like.

image.altText: A photo illustration showing a house with a 'For Sale' sign and an arrow above it representing the price of housing over time.

items.title: Albanese wants conference to 'showcase' Labor but not its AUKUS division

children.content: The prime minister declares the conference would be "a real opportunity to bring the whole Labor movement with us on our direction".

image.altText: Anthony Albanese

items.title: MP calls for investigation into anti-abortion campaigner

children.content: Anti-abortion campaigner Joanna Howe has been called out for potentially breaching campaign financing laws, with one MP labelling her "attack" style as harmful and dangerous.

image.altText: Joanna Howe stands next to women holding up ban feticide sign.

items.title: Serial harassers given '19 strikes' on Meta, eSafety boss says

children.content: Elon Musk's X Corp is fighting the eSafety Commissioner on three different take-down orders of violent content, including the killing of Charlie Kirk.

image.altText: A blonde woman in a teal suit.

children.content: Spain takes on Austria on Los Angeles for a place in the World Cup round of 16. Later on, Cristiano Ronaldo's Portugal faces Croatia and Switzerland does battle with Algeria. Follow live.

items.title: Spain takes the lead agianst Austria as Yamal dazzles
'It's so beautiful': Why the World Cup has us waking up to party with strangers

image.altText: Man wearing yellow Ghana soccer jersey blows into a vuvuzela inside a party environment

children.content: They were just supposed to be somewhere to watch the match, but World Cup viewing parties have ended up becoming so much more.

items.title: Childcare safety loophole for educators breaching regulations

children.content: A loophole in the government’s new childcare safety legislation allows educators who have breached safety standards to continue operating.

image.altText: A rubber giraffe-duck, teddy bear and various coloured hoops are seen among other toys.

items.title: Fuel security remains critical despite EV shift, Ampol boss says

children.content: Ampol's chief executive says the Iran conflict exposed Australia's fuel vulnerability and strengthened the case for keeping its remaining refinery operating.

image.altText: A close up of the exhaust pipes at a petrol refinery.

items.title: Kyiv 'night of horror' as at least 21 killed in Russian attacks across Ukraine

children.content: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy cut his Dublin trip short as the strikes trapped paramedics and drivers at an ambulance station.

image.altText: A building is damaged and on fire.

items.title: Former Olympian charged over alleged 'vandalism' of US Reflecting Pool

children.content: Former US Olympian David 'Davey' Hearn has been accused of "maliciously" breaking or destroying lining material on the bottom of the Washington DC pool, court documents show

image.altText: An aerial vision of the Washington DC reflecting pool, shown as a deep blue in marble framing alongside green trees.

items.title: Hype or reality? How Taylor Swift could transform the wedding industry

children.content: Fans believe they know the when and where of Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce's upcoming nuptials, but much of the reported two-day extravaganza will likely be a cloak-and-dagger operation.

items.title: Concussion awareness leaving parents 'terrified', experts say

children.content: A research paper published in June found almost a third of parents surveyed said they stopped their children from playing contact sports out of fear of concussion.

image.altText: View from a grandstand as boys in red jumpers run out onto a football field.

items.title: Vatican excommunicates members of right-wing breakaway group SSPX

children.content: The Vatican has excommunicated priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval.

image.altText: Pope Leo in gold and white robes holding up a golden goblet, over a stone platform with other silver goblets.

items.title: Maggie O'Farrell's 'angry' new novel reveals tragic toll of Ireland's Great Famine

children.content: The Academy Award-nominated writer dives into Irish history in Land, revealing how the Great Famine changed the country forever.

items.title: Is Tasmania ready for the AI data centre boom?

children.content: An artificial intelligence company wants to build three AI factories in Tasmania, but questions are being raised about the impacts and benefits for the state.

items.title: 'The best feeling in the world' for Lachie Kennedy is more than winning

children.content: Lachie Kennedy is a big fish in a relatively small pond, but the next few weeks will pit him against some of the world's best as he continues his rise in global athletics.

image.altText: Lachie Kennedy runs out past pyrotechnics at the Maurie Plant Meet.

items.title: Christian Brothers granted pause on abuse victim payouts

children.content: The Christian Brothers religious order has been granted a moratorium on all civil claims against it, after arguing it was running out of money to pay victims of child sexual abuse.

image.altText: cross on a church steeple at night

items.title: Gina Rinehart and estranged kids to pay large legal costs after royalties trial

children.content: Mining billionaire Gina Rinehart and her two estranged children will have to pay a large chunk of the legal costs able to be recovered from a mammoth trial that determined the payment of royalties from some of Australia's most lucrative iron ore mines, a WA judge has ruled.

image.altText: Gina Rinehart

items.title: 'Robbing people using visas': Applicants shocked by sudden price hike

children.content: International students and families say they feel shocked and exploited after visa fees across dozens of categories rose on July 1 without warning, with some surging more than 200 per cent.

image.altText: A man wearing glasses and a white chef's uniform is stir-frying at the stove.

items.title: De Minaur cruises through to Wimbledon third round with straight-sets win

children.content: Australian Alex de Minaur has raced into the third round at Wimbledon with a straight-sets victory over tricky French veteran Adrian Mannarino.

gallery.altText: Alex de Minaur running to the back of the court to chase a high lob.

items.title: UK PM apologises over forced adoptions, calls practice a 'stain on history'

children.content: UK PM Keir Starmer has formally apologised for the British state's role in separating tens of thousands of unmarried mothers from their babies.

image.altText: Keir Starmer sits in a room filled with women.

items.title: Privacy Commissioner ordered to hand over report on Amex security failures

children.content: The Privacy Commissioner has been ordered by parliament to hand over a secret report detailing its investigation into American Express's technology security failures, after using gag orders and legal threats to keep it confidential.

image.altText: Close-up photo of the square blue American Express logo on the corner of a yellow credit card.

items.title: Professor admits wanting university job to benefit his consulting firm

children.content: ICAC hears that John Dewar wanted his UOW appointment to benefit KordaMentha and his acknowledgement there could be a perception of a conflict of interest.

items.title: Eight Thai monks killed after 11yo crashes parent's truck into procession

children.content: An 11-year-old has driven his parents' truck into a Buddhist procession in Thailand, killing eight monks.

items.title: MP's company fined $555k over worker's quad bike death

children.content: The court heard the 82-year-old worker was not wearing a helmet and the quad had no rollover protection when the crash occurred.
